sections.title: Section II - Free Response
sections.directions: You have the indicated time to answer the following free response questions. It is not enough to answer a question by merely listing facts. You should present a cogent argument based on your critical analysis.

questions.prompt: ThegovernmentofanislandnationgrantsSkyRunnerAirlinesexclusivemonopolyrightstoservetheisland.SkyRunnerisearningpositiveeconomicprofits.(a)DrawacorrectlylabeledgraphforSkyRunner,andshoweachofthefollowing.(i)Theprofit−maximizingquantityoftickets,labeledQsubM(ii)Theprofit−maximizingpriceofaticket,labeledPsubM(iii)Thearearepresentingprofits,shadedcompletelyNowtheisland’stouristbureauasksthegovernmenttoconsiderthefollowingtwoproposals.•ProposalI:Setapriceceilingonticketsthateliminatesalldeadweightloss.•ProposalII:EliminateSkyRunner’smonopolyrights,whichwillremoveallbarrierstoentry.(b)SupposethegovernmentadoptsproposalI.Onyourgraphinpart(a),indicatethequantityofticketssoldintheshortrun,labeledQsubC.(c)SupposeinsteadthegovernmentadoptsproposalII.Howwilleachofthefollowingbeaffectedinthelongruncomparedtothemarketconditionsinpart(a)?(i)ThequantityofticketssoldbySkyRunner.Explain.(ii)ThepriceelasticityofdemandforSkyRunner’sairlineservice.Explain.(iii)SkyRunner’sprofits(iv)Thedeadweightlossinthemarket.Explain.BeginyourresponsetothisquestionatthetopofanewpageintheseparateFreeResponsebookletandfillintheappropriatecircleatthetopofeachpagetoindicatethequestionnumber.©2021CollegeBoard.VisitCollegeBoardontheweb:collegeboard.org.3__________________________________________________________AP®Microeconomics2021Free−ResponseQuestionsNumberofWorkersNumberofParkedCars0018220334445554660763
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: <span aria-label="Assume that Schmitt Inc. provides car parking services in a perfectly competitive output market and hires labor in a perfectly competitive input market. The market price per car parked is $10, the daily market wage per worker is $100, and fixed costs are $50 per day. The table above shows the number of workers required to park different quantities of cars per day. open parenaclose paren Calculate the marginal revenue product of the second worker. Show your work. open parenbclose paren How many workers will Schmitt Inc. hire to maximize profit? Relative to this number of hired workers, explain why Schmitt Inc. will not hire one additional worker. Your answer must use marginal analysis and numbers from the table. open parencclose paren Calculate the daily profit for Schmitt Inc. at the profit-maximizing quantity identified in part open parenbclose paren. Show your work. open parendclose paren Suppose new legislation requires each worker in the parking industry to purchase an individual insurance policy at the worker’s expense in order to legally park cars. open pareniclose paren Will the market wage paid by a typical firm in this industry increase, decrease, or stay the same in the long run? open pareniiclose paren For a typical firm in the industry, will the number of workers hired in the short run increase, decrease, or stay the same? Explain. Begin your response to this question at the top of a new page in the separate Free Response booklet and fill in the appropriate circle at the top of each page to indicate the question number. © 2021 College Board. Visit College Board on the web: collegeboard.org. 4 __________________________________________________________ AP® Microeconomics 2021 Free-Response Questions The figure presents a graph of two curves. The horizontal axis labeled “Bushels of Corn,” and the numbers 0 through 100, in increments of 10, are indicated. The vertical axis is labeled “Price per Bushel, in dollars,” and the numbers 0 through 10, in increments of 1, are indicated. One curve is labeled Supply and the other curve is labeled Demand. The Supply curve begins at the point with coordinates 0 comma 0, and moves upward and to the right in a straight line until it ends at the point with coordinates 90 comma 9. The Demand curve begins at the point with coordinates 0 comma 10, and moves downward and to the right in a straight line until it ends at the point with coordinates 90 comma 1. The two curves intersect at the point with coordinates 50 comma 5."><math><mrow><mi>A</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>S</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>h</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>I</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>c</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>p</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>v</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>g</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>v</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>f</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>l</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>v</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>h</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>l</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>f</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>l</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>v</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>T</mi><mi>h</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>s</mi><mo>$</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>l</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>w</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>g</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>w</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>k</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>s</mi><mo>$</mo><mn>100</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>f</mi><mi>i</mi><
